metadata.title: Diana Raluca | Psychology Practice
metadata.description: Professional psychology services offering EMDR, Mindfulness, NLP, and various therapeutic approaches for anxiety, depression, trauma, and more.

hero.description: We recognize that life presents complex challenges, and we are dedicated to assisting you in your journey towards a healthy mind. We provide fresh approaches to mental health care in a warm, open and non-judgmental environment.
hero.heroIntro: Providing professional mental health support in a warm, non-judgmental environment.

about.description: As a licensed psychotherapist (Heilpraktikerin für Psychotherapie), I am dedicated to guiding individuals toward mental wellness through evidence-based therapeutic approaches. With advanced knowledge in Schema Therapy, ACT, DBT, and training in IoPT 'Identity-Oriented Psychotrauma Theory' developed by Prof. Dr. Franz Ruppert through the Intention Method, I provide personalized care in a warm, supportive environment. I offer consultations in German, Romanian, and English.

compassionateCare.title: Compassionate Care
compassionateCare.description: Every session is approached with empathy, understanding, and genuine care for your wellbeing.

evidenceBased.title: Evidence-Based Methods
evidenceBased.description: Utilizing scientifically proven therapeutic approaches tailored to your unique needs.

confidential.title: Confidential & Safe
confidential.description: A secure, judgment-free environment where you can explore your thoughts and feelings freely.

about.vision: To achieve healthy minds through collaborative relationships and reflective practice
about.mission: To provide a safe and supportive environment that fosters collaborative relationships to help achieve the best outcomes and work towards healthy minds

philosophy.title: Our Philosophy
philosophy.description: We believe that mental health is not the absence of illness, but rather the resilience and ability to cope with life's challenges. We offer comprehensive psychological services including assessments, diagnosis, counseling, and psychotherapy.

approach.title: Our Approach
approach.description: We use conversation-based and evidence-based techniques in a warm, open, and non-judgmental environment. Each treatment plan is personalized to your unique needs.

services.description: Offering comprehensive psychological services for a wide range of conditions, using evidence-based therapeutic approaches.

anxiety.title: Anxiety Disorders
anxiety.description: Comprehensive treatment for generalized anxiety, panic attacks, social anxiety, and phobias using proven therapeutic techniques.

depression.title: Depression
depression.description: Evidence-based approaches to help you overcome depression, regain motivation, and rediscover joy in everyday life.

trauma.title: Trauma & PTSD
trauma.description: Specialized EMDR therapy and trauma-focused treatment to help process difficult experiences and find healing.

relationships.title: Relationship Issues
relationships.description: Individual and couples counseling to improve communication, resolve conflicts, and build stronger connections.

stress.title: Stress Management
stress.description: Learn effective coping strategies, mindfulness techniques, and lifestyle changes to manage chronic stress.

personalGrowth.title: Personal Growth
personalGrowth.description: Explore your potential, build self-esteem, and develop emotional intelligence for a more fulfilling life.

addiction.title: Addiction
addiction.description: Supportive treatment for substance and behavioral addictions, focusing on recovery and sustainable change.

eatingDisorders.title: Eating Disorders
eatingDisorders.description: Specialized care for anorexia, bulimia, binge eating, and other eating-related challenges.

perinatal.title: Perinatal Mood Disorders
perinatal.description: Support for pregnancy and postpartum mental health, including depression and anxiety.

grief.title: Grief & Loss
grief.description: Compassionate support through bereavement and life transitions.

bipolar.title: Bipolar Disorder
bipolar.description: Comprehensive management of mood episodes and long-term stability.

adhd.title: ADHD
adhd.description: Assessment and treatment for attention and hyperactivity challenges in adults and adolescents.

attachment.title: Attachment Disorder
attachment.description: Healing attachment wounds and building secure relationship patterns.

personality.title: Personality Disorders
personality.description: Specialized therapy for complex personality patterns and interpersonal difficulties.

sexualDysfunction.title: Sexual Dysfunctions
sexualDysfunction.description: Sensitive and professional support for sexual health concerns.

mensMentalHealth.title: Men's Mental Health
mensMentalHealth.description: Addressing unique mental health challenges faced by men in a supportive environment.

selfEsteem.title: Self-Esteem
selfEsteem.description: Building confidence and a positive self-image through therapeutic support.

sessionInfo.title: Session Information
sessionInfo.assessmentNote: Assessments and group programs may vary in duration
sessionInfo.languages: Sessions available in Romanian, German, and English

therapies.description: Each treatment plan is personalized, combining multiple therapeutic modalities to address your specific needs and goals.
therapies.bottomNote: Not sure which approach is right for you? During your initial consultation, we will discuss your concerns and create a personalized treatment plan together.

emdr.name: EMDR Therapy
emdr.description: Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing - a powerful treatment for trauma and PTSD that helps the brain process disturbing memories.
emdr.intro: It's known that severe emotional pain requires a long time to heal, and EMDR is a psychotherapy that enables us to heal from the symptoms and emotional distress that disturbing life experiences have caused. This type of therapy shows that the mind can in fact heal from psychological trauma just how the body recovers from physical trauma.
emdr.recognition: EMDR is recognised by the World Health Organisation (WHO) and the American Psychiatric Association (APA) as a Gold Standard treatment for PTSD. In 2018 the Australian Psychological Society (APS) included EMDR therapy as a Level I evidence-based psychological intervention for post-traumatic stress disorder in adults.
emdr.howItWorks: A Harvard researcher investigated why EMDR is effective and found that symptoms rooted in trauma were connected with the biological mechanisms involved in Rapid Eye Movement (REM) sleep. When EMDR therapy is conducted, the clinician helps the client enter a similar state that emulates REM sleep. This allows internal associations to arise and we can begin to process the memory and disturbing feelings. In successful EMDR therapy, the meaning of painful events is transformed on an emotional level.
emdr.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success
emdr.detailedDescription: EMDR is a psychotherapy that enables us to heal from the symptoms and emotional distress caused by disturbing life experiences. This type of therapy demonstrates that the mind can heal from psychological trauma just as the body recovers from physical trauma. EMDR is recognized as a Level I evidence-based intervention (Australian Psychological Society, 2018).

mindfulness.name: Mindfulness-Based Therapy
mindfulness.description: Integrate mindfulness practices into your healing journey, developing present-moment awareness and emotional regulation skills.
mindfulness.intro: Mindfulness is a state of being where we’re deliberately and completely aware of our thoughts, emotions and experiences, without distraction and judgement. In therapy, mindfulness allows us to become attuned to and connected with bodily sensations, cognitions and feelings, helping us to accept them without being controlled or heavily influenced by them.
mindfulness.howItHelps: It can become incredibly easy for our thoughts to accumulate and spiral – often without us being completely aware of the build up. Mindful practices – such as meditation – can be incorporated into therapies and aim to teach us how to break away from unhelpful or negative thought patterns that can cause us to spiral into a depressive state. It invites us to become aware of that accumulation, and allows us to separate ourselves from negative thought patterns, emotions, and bodily sensations that would otherwise become overwhelming or harmful.
mindfulness.conclusion: The state of awareness that we gain through mindfulness makes it easier to embrace other therapeutic strategies and emotional healing.
mindfulness.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success
mindfulness.detailedDescription: It can become incredibly easy for our thoughts to accumulate and spiral – often without us being completely aware of the build up. Mindful practices – such as meditation – can be incorporated into therapies and aim to teach us how to break away from unhelpful or negative thought patterns that can cause us to spiral into a depressive state. It invites us to become aware of that accumulation, and allows us to separate ourselves from negative thought patterns, emotions, and bodily sensations that would otherwise become overwhelming or harmful.

cbt.name: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy
cbt.description: A structured, goal-oriented approach that helps identify and change negative thought patterns and behaviors.
cbt.intro: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy is a structured, goal-oriented psychotherapy that focuses on examining the relationships between thoughts, feelings, and behaviors. By identifying and changing negative thought patterns, CBT helps individuals develop healthier ways of thinking and behaving.
cbt.howItWorks: CBT works by helping you become aware of inaccurate or negative thinking so you can view challenging situations more clearly and respond to them in a more effective way. The therapy typically involves efforts to change thinking patterns and behavioral patterns.
cbt.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success

whatToExpect.title: What to Expect
whatToExpect.description: CBT works by helping you become aware of inaccurate or negative thinking, so you can view challenging situations more clearly and respond to them more effectively. It is a structured therapy, usually short-term (12-20 sessions), with practical exercises between sessions.

nlp.name: NLP Techniques
nlp.description: Neuro-Linguistic Programming methods to transform limiting beliefs and create positive behavioral changes.
nlp.intro: NLP is used as a method of personal development through promoting skills, such as self-reflection, confidence, and communication. The technique to change thoughts and behaviors to help achieve desired outcomes for them. Is is also applied commercially to achieve work-orientated goals, such as improved productivity or job progression.
nlp.howItWorks: NLP uses perceptual, behavioral, and communication techniques to make it easier to change thoughts and actions. NLP detects and modifies unconscious biases or limitations of an individual's map of the world. It operates through the conscious use of language to bring about changes in thoughts and behavior.

benefits.title: NLP Uses Include
items: Treatment of phobias and generalized anxiety disorders
Improved psychological symptoms and life quality

techniques.title: Key NLP Techniques
items: Anchoring: Turning sensory experiences into triggers for certain emotional states
Rapport: Matching physical behaviors to improve communication and response through empathy
Swish pattern: Changing patterns of behavior or thought to achieve desired outcomes
Visual/kinesthetic dissociation (VKD): Removing negative thoughts and feelings associated with past events

nlp.vakog: NLP works with our five senses (VAKOG): Visual (seeing), Auditory (listening), Kinesthetic (feeling), Olfactory (smelling), and Gustatory (tasting). Our body, emotional life and thought systems influence each other - by changing your posture, you can change emotional states and thoughts.
nlp.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success
nlp.detailedDescription: NLP uses perceptual, behavioral, and communication techniques in order to  make it easier in changing thoughts and actions. NLP detects and modify unconscious biases or limitations of an individual’s map of the world. Is not hypnotherapy but it operates through the conscious use of language to bring about changes in thoughts and behavior. The framework involves rapport-building, information-gathering, and goal-setting.

family.name: Family Therapy
family.description: Address family dynamics, improve communication, and strengthen relationships within the family system.
family.fullTitle: Family Therapy for Couples and Families
family.intro: Family therapy for couples and families
family.approach: Family therapy is solution-oriented and strives to achieve rapid improvements. A key principle is 'helping clients to help themselves': Therapy encourages clients to take initiative in solving their problems. Therefore, a relatively short therapy period is often sufficient—on average, between 5 and 20 sessions.

benefits.title: Family Therapy Can Help With
items: Mental health problems of a child, adolescent, or adult
Chronic or life-threatening illness of a family member
Problems in foster care or adoption

family.advantages: A major advantage is that typical behavioral patterns, etiquette, and unwritten rules of the family can be directly observed during therapy sessions. The therapist's targeted questions enable new perspectives on problematic situations, and often minimal stimuli can initiate changes quickly, leading to surprising therapeutic successes.
family.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success

sessionInfo.title: About Sessions
sessionInfo.description: Family therapy is solution-oriented and strives for rapid improvements. A key principle is 'helping clients help themselves.' On average, between 5 and 20 sessions are sufficient.

family.uniqueAdvantage: A great advantage is that typical behavioral patterns, labels, and unwritten family rules can be observed directly during therapy sessions. The therapist's targeted questions allow for new perspectives and insights.
family.detailedDescription: A psychotherapeutic approach that focuses on the family as a social system. It initiates changes within the family system and supports the family in resolving difficulties, finding new perspectives, and developing more satisfying forms of coexistence.
family.howItHelps: A major advantage is that typical behavioral patterns, etiquette, and unwritten rules of the family can be directly observed during therapy sessions.The therapist's targeted questions enable new perspectives on the problematic situation, and often, with minimal stimuli, they can initiate changes relatively quickly, often leading to surprising therapeutic successes. Furthermore, this approach avoids blaming the patient or their family for a problem—because problems are always explained by the mutual influences between family members.

couples.name: Couples Counseling
couples.description: Build stronger connections, resolve conflicts, and develop healthy communication patterns with your partner.
couples.fullTitle: Family Therapy for Couples and Families
couples.intro: Family therapy for couples and families
couples.approach: Our approach avoids blaming either partner—problems are explained by mutual influences between partners. Through targeted questions and interventions, we help couples gain new perspectives and develop healthier patterns of interaction.
couples.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success
couples.detailedDescription: A psychotherapeutic approach that focuses on the family as a social system. It initiates changes within the family system and supports the family in resolving difficulties, finding new perspectives, and developing more satisfying forms of coexistence.
couples.howItHelps: A major advantage is that typical behavioral patterns, etiquette, and unwritten rules of the family can be directly observed during therapy sessions.The therapist's targeted questions enable new perspectives on the problematic situation, and often, with minimal stimuli, they can initiate changes relatively quickly, often leading to surprising therapeutic successes. Furthermore, this approach avoids blaming the patient or their family for a problem—because problems are always explained by the mutual influences between family members.

positivePsychotherapy.name: Positive and Transcultural Psychotherapy
positivePsychotherapy.description: A humanistic approach that integrates positive psychology with transcultural understanding for holistic healing.
positivePsychotherapy.intro: Positive and Transcultural Psychotherapy is a psychotherapeutic method of humanistic psychodynamic psychotherapy based on a positive conception of human nature. It is an integrative method that includes humanistic, systemic, psychodynamic and cognitive-behavioral elements.
positivePsychotherapy.goal: The main goal of PPT is to help identify and cultivate abilities, strengths, resources, and potentials. This approach combines elements from different modalities of psychotherapy.

elements.title: PPT Integrates
items: A humanistic perspective on human nature and therapeutic alliance
A psychodynamic understanding of mental and psychosomatic disorders
A systemic approach considering family, culture, work and environment

principles.title: The Three Main Principles of PPT

positivePsychotherapy.balanceModel: The balance model addresses biological-physical, rational-intellectual, socio-emotional, and imaginative-value-oriented aspects of daily life, including physical activities, professional achievements, relationships, and future plans.
positivePsychotherapy.applications: PPT is used to treat various mental health conditions including mood disorders, neurotic disorders, stress-related disorders, somatoform disorders, personality disorders, and behavioral syndromes. It is successful in individual therapy, couples, families and groups.
positivePsychotherapy.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success

systemicConstellation.name: Systemic Family Constellations
systemicConstellation.description: Reveal hidden dynamics in family systems and organizational structures for deeper understanding and resolution.
systemicConstellation.intro: Systemic Family & Business Constellations is a powerful, profound approach to addressing life’s challenges, going beyond the surface to connect with the matter at its source. It is a well-respected choice for those looking for sustainable results.
systemicConstellation.explanation: The unconscious patterns that affect our individual lives can also have an impact on our relationship and family lives. Not only do we need to uncover what these are, but once we have, to become clear about how to create a new context for relationship moving forward.
systemicConstellation.scope: This encompasses the impact of intergenerational trauma, where there is a pattern handed down from one generation to another or entrenched in a system, be it a family, institution or business. It can relate to behavioral and relationship patterns, as well as those associated with health, career, sexuality or money.

benefits.title: Benefits From This Approach
items: Remove the blocks to connection or commitment and be free to let love in
Move beyond the 'Blaming, Shaming & Naming Game' and lay the past to rest
Be more effective in being 'a united front' in parenting
Clarify the future of your relationship
Gain clarity about what led to a separation and co-parent with greater consciousness
Be more aware of hidden dynamics that impact on yourself and your partner
Gain clarity in business, leadership, teams, and organizations

systemicConstellation.business: Business constellations are a powerful method to analyze complex problems, find sustainable solutions, and free the energy to take necessary steps. They give in-depth insight and help establish the source of a problem in a short time.
systemicConstellation.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success
systemicConstellation.detailedDescription: The unconscious patterns that affect our individual lives can also have an impact on our Relationship & Family lives. Not only do we need to uncover what these are, but once we have, to become clear about how to create a new context for relationship moving forward and the skills and strategies to make amends where necessary to relate from a more heart-centered place.

solutionFocused.name: Solution-Focused Therapy
solutionFocused.description: A brief, goal-directed approach that focuses on solutions rather than problems to create rapid positive change.
solutionFocused.intro: Solution focused therapy concentrates on finding present-time solutions to problems and looking to our hopes for the future to come to a quick resolution. Solution focused therapy puts emphasis on the fact that we often already know what we need to do to improve our lives, but aims to help us realise and find the best solutions through coaching, questioning and goal setting.
solutionFocused.concept: This therapy is largely based around goal setting and the step-by-step process involved to reach our goals. It also aims to demonstrate to clients their resilience and the different ways they are capable of coping – many of which they are likely already using without acknowledgement.
solutionFocused.example: Our therapists could use solution focused therapy in multiple ways. One example would be asking you to describe your ideal scenario where your issues don't exist. This can help identify small and practical steps that can be immediately taken.
solutionFocused.applications: Solution focused therapy is often used to address issues for individuals, family units, and couples, but can also be useful to aid problems within a school or workplace.
solutionFocused.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success

miracleQuestion.title: The Miracle Question Technique
miracleQuestion.description: Our therapists might ask you to describe your ideal scenario where your problems don't exist. This can help identify small, practical steps that can be taken immediately.

identityTrauma.name: Identity-Oriented Trauma Therapy
identityTrauma.description: Specialized approach addressing how trauma affects identity and sense of self.
identityTrauma.intro: Healing from either physical or mental disorders needs a goal. According to Prof. Dr. Ruppert this "equates to fully and completely living one's own identity, so that we can express and realise what is at the core of our being". In order to be in touch with our own identity we "need a healthy self, a free will and to be in close emotional contact with our body".
identityTrauma.framework: The IoPT framework provides a safe way to explore the suppressed and unconscious parts of the psyche, to recover from the effects of trauma and to restore our healthy self. The central focus in this therapy is on personal life and an in-depth discovery of one's own identity.
identityTrauma.understanding: The framework shows how trauma affects the whole Living Human Organism (LHO) and how to recover from the effect of trauma to gain a healthy self and a healthy will, despite traumatisation. IoPT therapy is based on a specific understanding of trauma, and psychological splitting as the means of surviving trauma.

traumaBiography.title: Trauma Biography
traumaBiography.description: Every person has a trauma history, called a trauma biography. The traumas we go through have a major influence on who we become later in life and influence our relationships and abilities.

identityTrauma.keyInsight: Trauma is a situation that pays no attention to individual identity, and the cost is a loss of self, a loss of identity. This is why Dr. Ruppert's approach is called Identity-oriented Psychotrauma Theory.
identityTrauma.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success

keyPrinciples.title: Key IoPT Principles
items: Trauma is a situation that pays no attention to individual identity
The cost of trauma is a loss of self, a loss of identity
The IoPT framework offers a safe way to explore repressed parts of the psyche
Recovery involves gaining a healthy self and a healthy will
The Intention Method allows safe exploration and healing

identityTrauma.detailedDescription: The IoPT framework provides a safe way to explore the suppressed and unconscious parts of the psyche, to recover from the effects of trauma and to restore our healthy self. The central focus in this therapy is on a personal life and an in-depth discovery of one's own identity.

schemaTherapy.name: Schema Therapy
schemaTherapy.description: Integrative approach for deep-seated emotional patterns and personality-related issues.
schemaTherapy.intro: Schema Therapy is an integrative psychotherapy that combines elements of cognitive-behavioral therapy, attachment theory, and emotion-focused approaches. It was developed to treat chronic psychological problems and personality disorders that haven't responded well to other treatments.
schemaTherapy.schemas: Schemas are deep-seated emotional patterns that develop in childhood and continue throughout life. When triggered, they can lead to intense negative emotions and unhelpful behaviors. Schema Therapy helps identify these patterns and develop healthier ways of meeting emotional needs.
schemaTherapy.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success

schemaExplanation.title: What Are Schemas?
schemaExplanation.description: Schemas are deeply rooted emotional patterns that develop in childhood and continue throughout life. When triggered, they can lead to intense negative emotions and unhealthy behaviors. Schema Therapy combines elements from cognitive behavioral therapy, attachment theory, and emotion-focused approaches.

dbt.name: DBT
dbt.description: Dialectical Behavior Therapy combining cognitive-behavioral techniques with mindfulness practices.
dbt.intro: Dialectical Behavior Therapy is a comprehensive cognitive-behavioral treatment that was originally developed for individuals with borderline personality disorder. It combines standard cognitive-behavioral techniques with mindfulness practices derived from Buddhist meditative practices.

skills.title: DBT Teaches Four Main Skills
items: Mindfulness: Being fully present in the moment
Distress Tolerance: Coping with painful emotions in healthy ways
Emotion Regulation: Managing and changing intense emotions
Interpersonal Effectiveness: Communicating needs while maintaining relationships

dbt.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success

act.name: ACT
act.description: Acceptance and Commitment Therapy focusing on psychological flexibility and values-based living.
act.intro: ACT is a form of psychotherapy that uses acceptance and mindfulness strategies, together with commitment and behavior change strategies, to increase psychological flexibility. Rather than trying to eliminate difficult feelings, ACT teaches us to accept them as appropriate responses to certain situations.

principles.title: Six Core Processes of ACT
items: Acceptance: Allowing thoughts and feelings without struggling against them
Cognitive Defusion: Learning to perceive thoughts as just thoughts
Being Present: Awareness of the here and now
Self as Context: A sense of self that is unchanging and always present
Values: Discovering what is most important to you
Committed Action: Setting goals according to values and carrying them out

act.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success

systemicCounseling.name: Systemic Counseling
systemicCounseling.description: Exploring how relationships and systems influence emotional experience and behavior.
systemicCounseling.approach: Our approach is characterized by acceptance, empathy, impartiality, and appreciation. We assume the independence of the client and view them as 'experts in their own case.' Assuming that everyone can develop their own solutions, we work with the client's existing resources and skills.
systemicCounseling.principle: The client's resources are activated to achieve their individual solutions and goals as independently and self-organized as possible. The client is the expert in their problems and solutions; the counselor is the expert in the path to finding the solution.
systemicCounseling.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success
systemicCounseling.expertPrinciple: The client's resources are activated to achieve their individual solutions and goals as independently as possible. The client is the expert on their problems and solutions; the counselor is the expert on the path to finding the solution.
systemicCounseling.detailedDescription: Systemic counselors use conversations to explore how people's different relationships affect a system. Each social relationship influences a person's emotional experience and behavior in its own way.

bioenergetic.name: Bioenergetic Coaching
bioenergetic.description: A holistic method integrating body, mind, and soul for lasting change.
bioenergetic.explanation: Bioenergetic coaching, a holistic method, incorporates bioenergetic exercises aimed at activating the body and releasing stored tensions to promote inner balance and freedom. It combines thought reflection with physical activation and consideration of emotional and mental processes.

bodyMemory.title: Body Memory
bodyMemory.description: Body memory describes how the body stores experiences and emotions. Change processes cannot be controlled solely through thinking; they require the involvement of the body. The brain and body work closely together, and emotional blockages are anchored not just mentally, but also physically.

exercises.title: Simple Exercises to Try
items: Grounding: Stand with slightly bent knees, feet hip-width apart. Breathe deeply and notice how the floor supports you. Feel the contact of your feet with the ground and consciously release tension.
Shake to release tension: Stand upright, relax your knees, and gently shake your arms, shoulders, and legs. Breathe deeply and let go of daily stress.
Breathing exercise: With hands on your belly, breathe deeply and feel your belly expand. When exhaling, let the air escape slowly. This calms the mind and reduces stress.

bioenergetic.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success
bioenergetic.detailedDescription: Change doesn't just begin in the mind, but permeates the entire person—body, mind, and soul. As early as the 1950s, it was proven that emotional blockages are anchored not only mentally but also physically.

contact.description: Take the first step towards mental wellness. Reach out for a confidential consultation and discover how we can work together.
contact.locationRomania: Str. Poetului 1-3, Bl. R11, Ap.1, Arad, Romania

form.messagePlaceholder: Tell us briefly about what brings you here...
form.privacyNotice: Your information is confidential and will only be used to respond to your inquiry.
form.successMessage: Thank you for your message. We will contact you soon!

footer.brandDescription: Dipl. Psych. Raluca Diana Tocoian - Licensed Psychotherapist (Heilpraktikerin für Psychotherapie). Dedicated to guiding individuals toward mental wellness through evidence-based therapeutic approaches and compassionate care.
footer.accreditations: Accredited by CPR Romania | Recognized by BDP Germany | Member of WAPP, IoPT, NLP | Member VFP (Germany)

anxiety.title: Anxiety
anxiety.subtitle: Understanding and Overcoming Anxiety
anxiety.intro: Anxiety can occur in many people without warning and at any stage of our lives, and is often present alongside other disorders like depression or post-traumatic stress disorder. It’s an frequently diagnosed and treated mental health disorder, but at its core, feeling anxious is our body’s instinctive way of keeping us safe from danger.

symptoms.title: What Are 5 Symptoms Of Anxiety?
items: Intense feelings of worry, distress, nervousness, or panic
Feeling that these emotions are out of your control
A sense of being detached from your body
Catastrophizing (i.e., taking things out of proportion and experiencing thoughts like “everything is going to go wrong” or “I might die”)
Feeling tense, on edge, scared, agitated or nauseous

generalised_anxiety_disorder.name: Generalised Anxiety Disorder (GAD)
generalised_anxiety_disorder.description: Generalised anxiety is marked by excessive, exaggerated anxiety and worry about everyday life events out of your control

obsessive_compulsive_disorde.name: Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder (OCD)
obsessive_compulsive_disorde.description: Obsessions and compulsions is a mental illness that causes repeated unwanted thoughts or sensations (obsessions) or the urge to do something over and over again (compulsions)

social_anxiety.name: Social Anxiety
social_anxiety.description: The defining feature of social anxiety disorder (also called social phobia) is intense anxiety or fear of being judged or rejected in social or public situations

separation_anxiety.name: Separation Anxiety
separation_anxiety.description: This is a common type of anxiety experienced in children being separated from a parent or caregiver

panic_attacks_and_panic_diso.name: Panic Attacks And Panic Disorder
panic_attacks_and_panic_diso.description: An individual suffering from panic attacks will experience intense, overwhelming anxiety with physical symptoms like dizziness, fainting, excessive sweating, heart-racing and or chest pain.

agoraphobia.name: Agoraphobia
agoraphobia.description: A person with agoraphobia is typically fearful of leaving environments (like their home) that they consider to be safe

specific_phobias.name: Specific Phobias
specific_phobias.description: A specific phobia is experienced when a person feels an overwhelming fear of an object (like spiders, snakes or dogs) or a situation (e.g

health_anxiety.name: Health Anxiety
health_anxiety.description: An individual experiencing health anxiety typically experiences irrational worries about minor symptoms, falsely believing they indicate serious medical conditions

anxiety.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success
anxiety.detailedDescription: Anxiety can appear in many people without warning and at any stage of our lives, often appearing alongside other disorders such as depression or post-traumatic stress disorder. It is a commonly diagnosed and treated mental disorder, but essentially, the feeling of anxiety is our body's instinctive way of protecting us from danger.

triggers.title: What Triggers Anxiety?
triggers.description: Scenarios such as taking an exam or starting a new job can trigger anxiety. In these moments, anxiety can be beneficial, serving as a warning sign to be more attentive to things that could affect us. On the other hand, anxiety can also appear without a real threat and can intensify.
items: Major life changes (new job, relocation, divorce)
Social situations and evaluation by others
Loss of control over life situations

causes.title: Causes and Risk Factors
items: Chemical imbalances in the brain (serotonin, noradrenaline)
Traumatic experiences from childhood or adult life

treatmentApproach.title: Our Therapeutic Approach
treatmentApproach.description: We use a combination of evidence-based techniques to treat anxiety, tailored to your individual needs.
methods: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) for identifying and restructuring anxious thoughts
EMDR for processing traumatic experiences that fuel anxiety
Mindfulness and relaxation techniques for managing physical symptoms
Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT) for psychological flexibility
Breathing and grounding exercises for panic attacks

physicalSymptoms.title: Physical Symptoms of Anxiety
items: Difficulty breathing or feeling of suffocation

anxiety.whenToSeekHelp: It is important to seek professional help when worries and anxiety overwhelm you and negatively affect your daily life. Recognizing symptoms and learning effective coping mechanisms are essential steps toward recovery.

depression.title: DEPRESSION
depression.subtitle: Understanding Depression
depression.intro: It’s completely normal for our moods to shift and change as we respond and adapt to life’s many challenges. Feeling sad, moody or low is common, but some of us are unable to regulate our moods, or may feel intensely sad or low for a prolonged period of time. This can severely affect how we cope and live our day-to-day lives.

symptoms.title: Key Symptoms
items: Loss of interest in activities once enjoyed
Changes in appetite and sleep patterns

major_depression.name: Major Depression
major_depression.description: Major depression is sometimes called major depressive disorder, clinical depression, unipolar depression or simply ‘depression’

dysthymic_disorder.name: Dysthymic Disorder
dysthymic_disorder.description: The symptoms of dysthymia are similar to those of major depression but are less severe

bipolar_disorder.name: Bipolar Disorder
bipolar_disorder.description: This used to be known as ‘manic depression’ because the person experiences periods of depression and periods of mania, with periods of normal mood in between

perinatal_and_postnatal_depr.name: Perinatal And Postnatal Depression
perinatal_and_postnatal_depr.description: Women are at an increased risk of depression during pregnancy (known as the antenatal or prenatal period) and in the year following childbirth (known as the postnatal period)

seasonal_affective_disorder_.name: Seasonal Affective Disorder (SAD)
seasonal_affective_disorder_.description: SAD is a mood disorder that has a seasonal pattern

depression.statistics: Almost 10% of women experience depression during pregnancy; this increases to 16% in the first 3 months after birth. Bipolar disorder affects approximately 2% of the population.
depression.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success
depression.detailedDescription: It is completely normal for our moods to change as we respond to life's many challenges. Feeling sad or down is common, but some of us are unable to regulate our moods or feel very sad for long periods of time. Depression is much more than sadness - it includes a persistent feeling of dejection, worthlessness, numbness, or guilt that profoundly affects how we manage daily life.

causes.title: Causes and Risk Factors
items: Genetic predisposition and family history of depression
Social isolation and lack of emotional support

treatmentApproach.title: Our Therapeutic Approach
treatmentApproach.description: We offer a personalized treatment plan combining multiple therapeutic approaches.
methods: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for restructuring negative thoughts
Schema Therapy for deeply rooted emotional patterns
Behavioral activation for regaining motivation and pleasure
Mindfulness techniques for awareness and self-compassion

warningSignsDetailed.title: Detailed Warning Signs
warningSignsDetailed.description: Some people with depression appear to function normally, hiding behind a deceiving smile. These people often hide behind a mask of persistent dejection and fatigue.
items: Loss of interest in activities that once brought pleasure
Constant fatigue and lack of energy
Feelings of excessive guilt or worthlessness
Alcohol or substance use as a coping mechanism
Recurring thoughts about death or suicide

trauma.title: TRAUMA
trauma.subtitle: Understanding and Healing Trauma
trauma.intro: Frightening or disturbing events are distressing for anyone and can result in a psychological wound, known as trauma, which may be particularly exaggerated or triggering if you are already coping with existing mental illness.

types.title: What Are The 3 Types Of Trauma?

type1.name: Acute Trauma
type1.description: This is the most common type of trauma and is usually caused by a single, isolated event, such as a car accident or a natural disaster. The effects of acute trauma can be temporary, and most people will recover within a few days or weeks.

type2.name: Chronic Trauma
type2.description: This is the result of repeated or prolonged exposure to traumatic events, such as child abuse or domestic violence. The effects of chronic trauma can be long-lasting and severe, and can include physical, emotional, and psychological problems.

type3.name: Complex Trauma
type3.description: This is a combination of both acute and chronic trauma, and is often the result of prolonged exposure to multiple traumatic events, such as growing up in a war-torn country or experiencing multiple forms of abuse. The effects of complex trauma can be severe and long-lasting, and can include physical, emotional, and psychological problems.

trauma.ptsdNote: Trauma can develop into PTSD (Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder) when symptoms persist and significantly impact daily life.
trauma.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success
trauma.detailedDescription: Frightening or disturbing events are stressful for everyone and can lead to a psychological wound called trauma. This can be particularly intense if you are already dealing with an existing mental health condition. It could be a car accident, the loss of a loved one, violence, sexual assault or abuse, or a natural disaster. You can also be strongly affected if you witness someone else going through such a traumatic event.

causes.title: Common Causes of Trauma
items: Experiences in conflict zones or war
Intergenerational trauma passed down from parents or grandparents

treatmentApproach.title: Our Therapeutic Approach
treatmentApproach.description: We offer specialized trauma treatment using scientifically validated methods.
methods: EMDR - gold standard treatment recognized by WHO and APA for PTSD
Identity-Oriented Psychotrauma Therapy (IoPT) - specialized method developed by Prof. Dr. Franz Ruppert

ptsdDetailed.title: The Difference Between Trauma and PTSD
ptsdDetailed.description: It is important to note that while trauma and PTSD are related, they are not the same thing. PTSD is a condition that can develop after a traumatic event and is characterized by symptoms such as flashbacks, nightmares, and avoidance behavior. Trauma, on the other hand, is the event itself and can include a variety of physical, emotional, and psychological reactions.
ptsdSymptoms: Recurring nightmares related to the event
Avoidance of places, people, or situations associated with the trauma

addiction.title: ADDICTION
addiction.subtitle: Understanding and Overcoming Addiction
addiction.intro: Addiction can have a significant impact on mental health. It can lead to the development of mental health conditions such as depression, anxiety, and PTSD or can make existing mental health conditions worse, making it difficult for us to access appropriate help and support.

symptoms.title: What Are The Types Of Addiction?
items: Substance addiction: It means the use of illegal drugs or to use prescription or uncountable drugs or alcohol for other purposes than those for which they are meant to be used, mostly in excessive amounts. Substance abuse may lead to emotional, social, physical and job-related problems. Addiction to drugs and alcohol is the most well-known type of addiction. Another addictions can be: Marijuana, prescription medicines (pain pills), stimulants, anxiety pills, Methaamphetamine, cocaine, opiates, hallucinogens, inhalants.

Behavioural addiction: According to science this addiction is described as an intense desire to repeat some action that is pleasurable or perceived to improve wellbeing or capable of alleviating some personal distress. This includes addiction to activities such as gambling, sex, pornography, plastic surgery, gaming, food, exercise, and shopping.

Process addiction: It is an behavioral addiction characterized by an individual‘s preoccupation with certain activity, often compulsive that results in negative consequences. Addiction to things like food, the internet, texting, surfing the web, overeating and video games.
Relational addiction: Addiction to relationships which often looks like codependency. One person ‘‘love‘‘ another person in an obsessive intensity that is not in the interest of either party.
Trauma addiction: Addiction to the emotional and physical sensations associated with traumatic experiences.

types.title: What Are The Types Of Addiction?

type1.name: Substance addiction
type1.description: It means the use of illegal drugs or to use prescription or uncountable drugs or alcohol for other purposes than those for which they are meant to be used, mostly in excessive amounts. Substance abuse may lead to emotional, social, physical and job-related problems. Addiction to drugs and alcohol is the most well-known type of addiction. Another addictions can be: Marijuana, prescription medicines (pain pills), stimulants, anxiety pills, Methaamphetamine, cocaine, opiates, hallucinogens, inhalants.

type2.name: Behavioural addiction
type2.description: According to science this addiction is described as an intense desire to repeat some action that is pleasurable or perceived to improve wellbeing or capable of alleviating some personal distress. This includes addiction to activities such as gambling, sex, pornography, plastic surgery, gaming, food, exercise, and shopping.

type3.name: Process addiction
type3.description: It is an behavioral addiction characterized by an individual‘s preoccupation with certain activity, often compulsive that results in negative consequences. Addiction to things like food, the internet, texting, surfing the web, overeating and video games.

type4.name: Relational addiction
type4.description: Addiction to relationships which often looks like codependency. One person ‘‘love‘‘ another person in an obsessive intensity that is not in the interest of either party.

type5.name: Trauma addiction
type5.description: Addiction to the emotional and physical sensations associated with traumatic experiences.

addiction.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success
addiction.detailedDescription: Addiction can have a significant impact on mental health. It can lead to the development of mental health conditions such as depression, anxiety, and PTSD, or can worsen existing mental health conditions. It is important to note that many who struggle with addiction also face mental health issues, and it is essential to address both problems simultaneously.

causes.title: Causes of Addiction
causes.description: While the causes of addiction are complex and different for each person, research suggests that genetic, environmental, and personal factors play a role in the development of addiction.
items: Genetic predisposition - can make a person more vulnerable to addiction
Environmental factors - stress, trauma, access to substances
Pre-existing mental health conditions (depression, anxiety, PTSD)
Unhealthy coping mechanisms developed in childhood
Social isolation and lack of emotional support

treatmentApproach.title: Our Therapeutic Approach
methods: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for identifying and changing thought patterns
Trauma-focused therapy for addressing underlying causes
Mindfulness techniques for managing cravings and impulses
Dual approach - simultaneously treating addiction and associated mental health conditions

adhd.title: What Is ADHD?
adhd.subtitle: Understanding ADHD
adhd.intro: ADHD is one of the most common neurodevelopmental disorders of childhood. It’s usually first diagnosed in childhood and often lasts into adulthood.

symptoms.title: ADHD Symptoms: What Are Common Daily Challenges Adults With ADHD May Experience?

type1.name: Short attention span for age (difficulty sustaining attentio

adhd.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success

treatmentApproach.title: Our Therapeutic Approach
methods: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy adapted for ADHD

attachment.title: ATTACHMENT DISORDER
attachment.subtitle: Understanding Attachment Issues
attachment.intro: Experiencing attachment issues can be quite common for adults and while there is no formal diagnosis, it’s often thought to stem from untreated attachment issues from one’s childhood. If you don’t work to resolve attachment issues, you may go through life struggling to trust others and feel safe and secure in an intimate relationship.

symptoms.title: Key Signs
items: Difficulty trusting others or forming close relationships

secure_attachment.name: Secure Attachment
secure_attachment.description: Are you comfortable expressing emotions openly? Are you comfortable depending on your partner? And letting your partner depend on you? Then it’s likely you have a secure attachment style

attachment_anxiety.name: Attachment Anxiety
attachment_anxiety.description: Do you highly value your relationships but often worry whether your loved one is as invested in the relationship as you are? You may suffer from attachment anxiety

avoidant_or_dismissive_attac.name: Avoidant Or Dismissive Attachment
avoidant_or_dismissive_attac.description: Do you tend to hide or suppress your feelings when faced with an emotion-dense situation? If you feel you are emotionally strong, independent and self-sufficient, and often see yourself as a ‘lone wolf’, you may have an avoidant attachment style

disorganised_or_fearful_atta.name: Disorganised Or Fearful Attachment
disorganised_or_fearful_atta.description: If you show socially unstable or ambiguous behaviours, don’t seek intimacy or closeness and find it hard to trust and depend on others, you may have a fearful attachment style

attachment.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success
attachment.detailedDescription: Attachment disorders develop when early bonds with caregivers are disrupted or absent. Attachment patterns form in childhood and profoundly influence relationships, emotional regulation, and self-worth throughout life.

secure.name: Secure Attachment
secure.description: Are you comfortable expressing emotions openly? Are you comfortable depending on your partner? And letting your partner depend on you? Then it’s likely you have a secure attachment style

anxious.name: Attachment Anxiety
anxious.description: Do you highly value your relationships but often worry whether your loved one is as invested in the relationship as you are? You may suffer from attachment anxiety

avoidant.name: Avoidant Or Dismissive Attachment
avoidant.description: Do you tend to hide or suppress your feelings when faced with an emotion-dense situation? If you feel you are emotionally strong, independent and self-sufficient, and often see yourself as a ‘lone wolf’, you may have an avoidant attachment style

disorganized.name: Disorganised Or Fearful Attachment
disorganized.description: If you show socially unstable or ambiguous behaviours, don’t seek intimacy or closeness and find it hard to trust and depend on others, you may have a fearful attachment style

longTermEffects.title: Long-Term Effects
items: Substance use as a coping mechanism

treatmentApproach.title: Our Therapeutic Approach
methods: Emotion-focused therapy for healing attachment wounds

eatingDisorders.title: EATING DISORDERS
eatingDisorders.subtitle: Understanding Eating Disorders
eatingDisorders.intro: Eating disorders affect many people in the world. According to the statistics this significant global health concern shows that at least 9% of population will experience an eating disorder at some point in their lives. It is very important to understand and address disordered eating. They are a complex mental health conditions that require professional interventions.

symptoms.title: Warning Signs
items: Preoccupation with food, weight, or body shape

types.title: What Are Three Common Types Of Eating Disorders?

anorexia_nervosa.name: Anorexia Nervosa
anorexia_nervosa.description: This involves the extreme restriction of food intake, leading to dangerously low body weight

bulimia_nervosa.name: Bulimia Nervosa
bulimia_nervosa.description: Quite the opposite of anorexia, bulimia usually involves a cycle of binge eating followed by the compensation of purging, fasting or excessive exercise

binge_eating.name: Binge Eating
binge_eating.description: This involves recurrent episodes of consuming large amounts of food in a short period of time, followed by feelings of shame, guilt and distress

eatingDisorders.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success
eatingDisorders.detailedDescription: Eating disorders affect many people worldwide. Statistics show that at least 9% of the population will suffer from an eating disorder at some point in their lives. They are complex mental health conditions that require professional intervention.

causes.title: Causes and Risk Factors
items: Social pressure and unrealistic beauty standards
Low self-esteem and negative body image
Pressure from sports or professional activities
Influence of social media and media

treatmentApproach.title: Our Therapeutic Approach
treatmentApproach.description: Recovery from an eating disorder is absolutely possible with proper treatment.
methods: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy adapted for eating disorders
Schema Therapy for underlying emotional patterns
Mindful eating techniques and body reconnection
Working with body image and self-esteem
Nutritional support integrated into the therapeutic process

grief.title: GRIEF & LOSS
grief.subtitle: Understanding Grief
grief.intro: Loss and grief are universal experiences and a very complex emotion that affect us all at some point in our lives. Whether it’s the loss of a loved one, a job, or even a cherished possession, the emotions that come with these losses can be overwhelming and difficult to navigate.

symptoms.title: What Are The 7 Stages Of Grief After A Death?

acute.name: Acute Grief
acute.description: Intense grief immediately following a loss.

complicated.name: Complicated Grief
complicated.description: Prolonged grief that interferes with daily functioning.

anticipatory.name: Anticipatory Grief
anticipatory.description: Grief experienced before an expected loss.

disenfranchised.name: Disenfranchised Grief
disenfranchised.description: Grief that is not openly acknowledged or socially supported.

grief.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success
grief.detailedDescription: Loss and grief are universal experiences and a very complex emotion that affects us all at some point in life. Whether it is the loss of a loved one, a job, or even an important possession, the emotions that accompany these losses can be overwhelming and difficult to manage.

effects.title: Effects of Grief and Loss
effects.description: The effects of grief can be significant and far-reaching. Grief can affect our physical, emotional, and mental wellbeing.

stages.title: The 7 Stages of Grief

items.description: The initial reaction of disbelief and emotional numbness
Intense feelings of suffering and self-blame
Frustration and attempts to change reality
The beginning of the acceptance process
Integrating the loss and opening to the future

stages.note: It is important to note that grief is different for everyone and some of us may not experience all of these stages or may experience them in a different order.

treatmentApproach.title: Our Therapeutic Approach
methods: Empathic support and safe space for expressing emotions
Techniques for processing pain and loss
Support for complicated or prolonged grief

relationships.title: RELATIONSHIPS
relationships.subtitle: Understanding Relationship Challenges
relationships.intro: Relationships are the basis of our lives. They help us feel safe and challenged, and help us learn and grow.

symptoms.title: What Are The 4 Main Types Of Relationships?
items: Romantic Relationships: Romantic relationships are perhaps the most well-known type of relationship. They involve a deep emotional and physical connection between two people and often include a strong commitment to one another. Romantic relationships can take many forms, including dating, engagement, and marriage. They can be long-term or short-term and are usually characterised by love, trust, and mutual support.

Platonic Relationships: These are non-romantic relationships between friends. They are built on trust, mutual respect, and shared interests. Platonic relationships can be close and intimate, but they do not involve a romantic or sexual connection. Examples of platonic relationships include friendships between coworkers, classmates, or neighbours.

Family Relationships: These relationships are often characterised by a strong sense of loyalty, love, and responsibility. Familial relationships can be complex, with many different dynamics at play. They can include relationships between parents and children, siblings, grandparents, and other relatives.

Professional Relationships: These exist between people who work together and are often characterised by a shared goal or purpose and can include relationships between colleagues, supervisors, and subordinates. They can be formal or informal, and they can be based on trust, respect, and mutual support.

types.title: What Are The 4 Main Types Of Relationships?

type1.name: Romantic Relationships
type1.description: Romantic relationships are perhaps the most well-known type of relationship. They involve a deep emotional and physical connection between two people and often include a strong commitment to one another. Romantic relationships can take many forms, including dating, engagement, and marriage. They can be long-term or short-term and are usually characterised by love, trust, and mutual support.

type2.name: Platonic Relationships
type2.description: These are non-romantic relationships between friends. They are built on trust, mutual respect, and shared interests. Platonic relationships can be close and intimate, but they do not involve a romantic or sexual connection. Examples of platonic relationships include friendships between coworkers, classmates, or neighbours.

type3.name: Family Relationships
type3.description: These relationships are often characterised by a strong sense of loyalty, love, and responsibility. Familial relationships can be complex, with many different dynamics at play. They can include relationships between parents and children, siblings, grandparents, and other relatives.

type4.name: Professional Relationships
type4.description: These exist between people who work together and are often characterised by a shared goal or purpose and can include relationships between colleagues, supervisors, and subordinates. They can be formal or informal, and they can be based on trust, respect, and mutual support.

relationships.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success
relationships.detailedDescription: Relationships are the foundation of our lives. They help us feel safe and challenged, and help us learn and grow. Every healthy and close relationship will generate conflicts and disagreements - it is our way of managing differences in ideas, beliefs, and perspectives. When conflict becomes chronic, it can lead to serious stress and, if not managed, can contribute to the deterioration of mental and physical health.

items.name: Romantic Relationships
items.description: Involve a deep emotional and physical connection between two people. They can take many forms: dating, engagement, marriage. They are characterized by love, trust, and mutual support.

items.name: Platonic Relationships
items.description: Non-romantic relationships between friends, based on trust, mutual respect, and shared interests.

items.name: Family Relationships
items.description: Marked by a strong sense of loyalty, love, and responsibility. They can be complex with many different dynamics.

items.name: Professional Relationships
items.description: Existing between people who work together, characterized by a common purpose or goal.

stress.title: STRESS
stress.subtitle: Understanding and Managing Stress
stress.intro: Stress is a common issue that affects many people. It’s a natural response to challenging situations, but when it becomes chronic, it can have a negative impact on both physical and mental health.

symptoms.title: What Are The 7 Symptoms Of Stress?
items: Bursts of anger, irritability or sadness
A loss in sense of humour

acute.name: Acute Stress
acute.description: Short-term stress from immediate pressures or demands.

chronic.name: Chronic Stress
chronic.description: Long-term stress from ongoing situations or circumstances.

traumatic.name: Traumatic Stress
traumatic.description: Stress resulting from exposure to traumatic events.

burnout.name: Burnout
burnout.description: Exhaustion from prolonged work-related or caregiving stress.

stress.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success
stress.detailedDescription: Stress is a common problem that affects many people. It is a natural reaction to challenging situations, but when it becomes chronic, it can negatively affect physical and mental health.

causes.title: The Most Common Causes of Stress

items.name: Work-related stress
items.description: According to the APA, work is the primary source of stress in adults. Over 60% of employees experience work-related stress. Common causes include long working hours, tight deadlines, lack of control, and insufficient support.

items.name: Relationship stress
items.description: Stress in personal relationships can have various causes: conflicts with family members, friends, or romantic partners, financial problems, or lack of social support.

items.name: Financial stress
items.description: Financial problems are a major source of stress. According to APA studies, 72% of adults reported that money is a significant source of stress.

physicalEffects.title: Physical Effects of Stress
physicalEffects.description: When we are stressed, our bodies release cortisol and adrenaline, which in the short term can be helpful but in the long term can cause serious health problems.

treatmentApproach.title: Our Therapeutic Approach
methods: Mindfulness and meditation for stress reduction
Strategies for a healthy work-life balance
Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for changing stressful thought patterns

selfCare.title: Some Of The Ways We Can Help You Overcome Stress Include:
items: Understanding ways to achieve a healthy work/life balance
Utilising proven strategies and techniques that effectively manage stress

perinatal.title: PERINATAL MOOD DISORDERS
perinatal.subtitle: Understanding Perinatal Mental Health
perinatal.intro: While every experience is different, having a baby is a significant life event. Much like the physical changes we go through during pregnancy, there may be many emotional changes that come with each trimester. While it’s a time of joy, excitement and many firsts, it can also come with a degree of anxiety or ‘ups and downs’ in mood. Although it is normal to experience these emotions when expecting a baby, some of us do develop more intense levels of anxiety or lowered moods which can impact our daily lives. When this occurs during pregnancy it is known as antenatal anxiety or antenatal depression.

symptoms.title: Signs Of Perinatal Depression
items: Persistent worry about the health or wellbeing of your baby
Constantly feeling low, hopeless or empty
Common sadness or crying (for no obvious reason)
Intrusive thoughts and compulsive behaviours (e.g., unwanted thoughts relating to harming your baby)

type1.name: A pre-existing history of anxiety and/or depression

type2.name: Previous reproductive loss (infertility, IVF, miscarriage, t

perinatal.statistics: Almost 10% of women experience depression during pregnancy; this increases to 16% in the first 3 months after birth.
perinatal.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success
perinatal.detailedDescription: Perinatal mood disorders affect parents during pregnancy and the postpartum period. 1 in 5 expectant mothers experience perinatal depression or anxiety. 1 in 20 expectant fathers experience depression. These conditions are treatable with proper support.

treatmentApproach.title: Our Therapeutic Approach
methods: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy adapted for the perinatal period
Support for creating the mother-child bond
Couples counseling for the transition to parenthood

sexualDysfunction.title: SEXUAL DYSFUNCTIONS
sexualDysfunction.subtitle: Understanding Sexual Health
sexualDysfunction.intro: Sexual dysfunction refers to conditions that impact sexual desire, arousal, orgasm, and pain during intercourse. It is a problem that can happen during any phase of the sexual cycle and prevents from experiencing satisfaction from sexual activity. The sexual response cycle traditionally includes excitement, plateau, orgasm and resolution. Sexual dysfunction is common which is why people of all sexes, genders, orientations, and relationship statuses can benefit from psychosexual therapy.

symptoms.title: What Are Other Sexual Dysfunction Examples?
items: Low mood contributing to low sexual desire
Adjusting to sex after pregnancy and childbirth

desire.name: Desire Disorders
desire.description: Low or absent sexual desire affecting quality of life.

arousal.name: Arousal Difficulties
arousal.description: Challenges with physical or emotional arousal.

sexualDysfunction.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success
sexualDysfunction.detailedDescription: Sexual dysfunctions can affect all phases of the sexual response cycle - desire, arousal, orgasm. They affect people of all genders and orientations. With sensitive professional support, many of these challenges can be successfully addressed.

causes.title: Causes and Risk Factors
items: Physical illnesses and medication side effects
Relationship problems and lack of communication

treatmentApproach.title: Our Therapeutic Approach
methods: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for performance anxiety
Couples counseling for improving intimate communication
Specific exercises for improving sexual function

mensMentalHealth.title: MEN’S MENTAL HEALTH
mensMentalHealth.subtitle: Supporting Men's Wellbeing
mensMentalHealth.intro: There’s no denying that mental instability in men is also common.  Men can develop most of the same mental disorders and conditions like women, but they may experience different symptoms. In the nowadays silent epidemic in society which is impacting families, teachers, community members and friends, unfortunately the rate of male depression have dramatically risen.

symptoms.title: Common Challenges
items: Anger or irritability as primary emotional expression
Physical symptoms like headaches or digestive issues

depression.name: Male Depression
depression.description: Depression in men often presents differently and may go unrecognized.

anger.name: Anger Management
anger.description: Understanding and managing anger in healthy ways.

identity.name: Identity & Purpose
identity.description: Exploring masculinity, roles, and life purpose.

mensMentalHealth.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success
mensMentalHealth.detailedDescription: Men often present different symptoms than women for the same conditions. Male depression rates have increased dramatically. Societal pressure to suppress emotions and the stigma associated with men's mental health cause many men to delay seeking professional help.

causes.title: Causes and Risk Factors
items: Societal pressure to be 'strong' and not show vulnerability
Emotional isolation and lack of social support

selfCare.title: Self-Care Strategies for Men
items: Time spent in nature and outdoor activities

treatmentApproach.title: Our Therapeutic Approach
treatmentApproach.description: We offer a safe and non-judgmental space where men can explore their mental health challenges.
methods: Individual therapy adapted to men's specific needs
Anger management and emotional regulation techniques
Working with masculine identity and gender roles

bipolar.title: BIPOLAR DISORDER
bipolar.subtitle: Understanding Bipolar Disorder
bipolar.intro: To outsiders looking in, bipolar disorder can be hard to recognise as it comes in many forms. It’s a complex disorder, and people living with bipolar disorder will have periods of depression and mania and will alternate between these two extremes.

symptoms.title: Key Symptoms
items: Periods of unusually elevated mood or energy
Decreased need for sleep during manic episodes

types.title: What Are The Types Of Bipolar Disorder?

bipolar_i_disorder.name: Bipolar I Disorder
bipolar_i_disorder.description: Manic episodes will typically last at least one week

bipolar_ii_disorder.name: Bipolar II Disorder
bipolar_ii_disorder.description: This disorder is made up of a pattern of depressive episodes and hypomanic manic episodes (less severe than manic episodes), without ever having a full manic episode

cyclothymic_disorder.name: Cyclothymic Disorder
cyclothymic_disorder.description: Multiple episodes of manic and depressive symptoms lasting for at least two years, though not meeting the full diagnostic criteria of hypomanic or depressive episodes.

bipolar.statistics: Bipolar disorder affects approximately 2% of the population.
bipolar.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success
bipolar.detailedDescription: Bipolar disorder, previously known as 'manic depression,' involves periods of depression and mania, with periods of normal mood in between. Mania is the opposite of depression and can vary in intensity. Bipolar disorder appears to be most closely linked to family history.

causes.title: Causes and Risk Factors
items: Strong genetic predisposition and family history
Stress and conflicts can trigger episodes
Can be misdiagnosed as depression, ADHD, or schizophrenia

treatmentApproach.title: Our Therapeutic Approach
methods: Psychoeducation about bipolar disorder and symptom management
Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for managing episodes
Mood monitoring and identifying warning signs

personality.title: PERSONALITY DISORDERS
personality.subtitle: Understanding Personality Patterns
personality.intro: A personality disorder is a mental disorder in which we experience a rigid and unhealthy pattern of thinking and behaving. Suffering from a personality disorder, is often experience challenges perceiving and relating to other people and situations. This causes significant problems in forming and maintaining positive relationships, work, school, and social life.

symptoms.title: Common Features
items: Patterns of thinking that cause problems

cluster_a.name: Cluster A
cluster_a.description: People with ‘Cluster A’ personality disorders typically experience ‘odd’ or ‘eccentric’ thoughts or behaviors:

cluster_b.name: Cluster B
cluster_b.description: People with ‘Cluster B’ personality disorders typically experience unstable emotions and dramatic or impulsive behaviours:

cluster_c.name: Cluster C
cluster_c.description: People with ‘Cluster C’ personality disorders typically experience anxious and fearful thoughts and behaviour:

personality.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success

treatmentApproach.title: Our Therapeutic Approach
methods: Schema Therapy - specialized approach for personality disorders
DBT (Dialectical Behavior Therapy) - especially for Borderline

personality.detailedDescription: In some cases, people may not realise having a personality disorder because the way of thinking and behaving seems natural to them, or may even blame others for the challenges they have.

causes.title: What Is The Root Cause Of Personality Disorders?
items: Currently, the causes of personality disorders aren’t entirely understood.
Current studies show that the development of a personality disorder is due to a combination of early childhood environment and genetics.
People with particular personality types, such as borderline personality disorders (BPD), are known to have higher rates of childhood abuse, neglect or/and trauma.
Personality disorders usually develop in teenage or early adulthood years..

selfEsteem.title: SELF-ESTEEM
selfEsteem.subtitle: Building Healthy Self-Worth
selfEsteem.intro: In today’s world, people are overloaded with messages about how they should look and behave, which is why self-esteem is something that many of people struggle with. But maintaining a positive self image is critical for the own mental health and overall well-being.

symptoms.title: Signs of Low Self-Esteem
items: Fear of failure or taking risks

selfWorth.name: Core Self-Worth
selfWorth.description: Developing a stable sense of inherent value.

selfCompassion.name: Self-Compassion
selfCompassion.description: Learning to treat yourself with kindness and understanding.

confidence.name: Confidence Building
confidence.description: Developing belief in your abilities and potential.

boundaries.name: Healthy Boundaries
boundaries.description: Learning to value and protect your needs.

selfEsteem.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success

treatmentApproach.title: Our Therapeutic Approach
methods: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for restructuring negative thoughts
Schema Therapy for deeply rooted patterns

impressum.title: Impressum
impressum.copyrightText: All content created by site operators is subject to German copyright law. No reproduction without written permission.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: We take your privacy seriously. This policy describes how we collect, use, and protect your personal information.

whyDifferent.title: Why I Am Different
whyDifferent.tagline: I BELIEVE YOUR MENTAL HEALTH MATTERS
points: Priorities client wellbeing and addresses overlooked issues
Looks at 8 forms of intelligence (naturalistic, interpersonal, intrapersonal, linguistic, logic-mathematical, bodily/kinesthetic, musical, spatial)
Creates professional, personal, compassionate wellbeing experience
whyDifferent.cta: Heal, Restore and Thrive - We remodel your boundaries and success

detailedPoints.title: Holistic Approach
detailedPoints.description: I prioritize client wellbeing and address issues overlooked by other services. We don't just treat symptoms, but the whole person.

detailedPoints.title: Continuous Feedback
detailedPoints.description: I continuously review client feedback to adapt and constantly improve the therapeutic process.

detailedPoints.title: Eight Forms of Intelligence
detailedPoints.description: I analyze and leverage the 8 forms of intelligence: naturalistic, interpersonal, intrapersonal, linguistic, logical-mathematical, bodily/kinesthetic, musical, and spatial.

detailedPoints.title: Personalized Experience
detailedPoints.description: I create a professional, personal, and compassionate wellbeing experience tailored to each individual client.

detailedPoints.title: Skills for Change
detailedPoints.description: I provide concrete skills and practical tools for meaningful and lasting change in your life.

assessment.title: Pre-Consultation Assessment
assessment.subtitle: Help us understand your needs
assessment.intro: This confidential assessment helps us prepare for your consultation. Please answer honestly - there are no right or wrong answers.
assessment.personalInfoDesc: Please provide your contact details so we can reach you.
assessment.checklistDesc: Please select any items that apply to you. This information helps us better understand your situation.
assessment.healthyIdentityDesc: These represent positive traits. Select any that you feel describe you.
assessment.additionalInfoDesc: Is there anything else you would like us to know before your consultation?
assessment.notesPlaceholder: Share any additional information that might help us understand your situation better...
assessment.privacyNotice: Your information is strictly confidential and will only be used to prepare for your consultation. We comply with GDPR and German data protection laws.
assessment.successMessage: Thank you for completing the assessment. We will review your responses and contact you soon to schedule your consultation.
assessment.error: An error occurred. Please try again.
